risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Randomization to MBSR or the health education control was performed using a random number generator. Personnel in the Penn State Hershey Medical Center Department of Public Health Sciences used Statistical Analysis System software version 9.2 proc plan to create a list based on permuted-blocks randomization scheme with subjects equally allocated to the two arms. Randomization to MBSR or health education control was stratified based on PCOS status (PCOS or non-PCOS)." Comment: The paper explicitly describes a proper random sequence generation method (random number generator/computer software), with additional detail on permuted blocks and stratification, which supports truly random allocation.

Quote: "Randomization to MBSR or the health education control was performed using a random number generator... Personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences communicated the randomization scheme to the Class Scheduler who is not involved in the collection or review of outcomes data. The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors." Comment: The report describes who generated/performed randomization and that outcome assessors were blinded, but it does not explicitly describe a concealment mechanism that prevented the person enrolling participants from foreseeing assignments before enrollment (e.g. central allocation, web/telephone system, or sequentially numbered opaque sealed envelopes). Therefore allocation concealment is unclear.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The Class Scheduler communicated the study group assignments to the subjects and the instructors." and "Eighty six (86) women with BMI ≥25 kg/m2, including 31 women with PCOS, were randomized to one of two groups for 8 weeks and followed for 16 weeks: (1) MBSR, or (2) health education control." Quote: "The primary outcome is the Toronto Mindfulness Scale (TMS)" and major secondary outcomes include "Short Form-36 (SF-36) as a measure of health-related quality of life, and Brief Symptom Inventory 18 (BSI-18) as a measure of overall psychological distress." Comment: Participants and intervention personnel/instructors were explicitly informed of group assignment, so blinding of participants and personnel was not present. Because the study's dominant outcomes include self-reported mindfulness, quality of life, psychological distress, perceived stress and similar subjective outcomes, knowledge of allocation could plausibly influence outcomes.

Quote: "Randomization to MBSR or the health education control was performed by personnel in the Department of Public Health Sciences, and was not be communicated to research personnel who are collecting and reviewing outcomes data... The principal investigator, study coordinator and all study personnel involved in the collection and review of outcomes data are blinded." and "All questionnaires were self-administered questionnaires that were reviewed by blinded study personnel for completeness... Study personnel collecting measurements were blinded." Comment: Outcome assessors included patient self-report for questionnaires (e.g. TMS, SF-36, BSI-18, PROMIS, PSS-10), blinded study personnel for physical/clinician-assessed measures, and lab-based systems for blood assays. Although the study includes subjective self-reported outcomes, the report explicitly states that assignments were concealed from subjects and that subjects were informed only that the study compared two stress-reduction programs, reducing the likelihood that self-reported outcome assessment was influenced by allocation knowledge. Blinded study personnel collected/reviewed outcomes, and objective laboratory outcomes are unlikely to be biased.

Quote: "Eighty-six (86) women, including 31 women with PCOS, have been randomized to MBSR or health education control for 8 weeks and followed for 16 weeks." and "Data analysis will follow the principle of intent-to-treat (ITT) regardless of subject compliance with the sessions... linear mixed-effects models provide greater flexibility in case of missing data." Comment: The paper reports numbers randomized (42 MBSR, 44 control) and planned follow-up duration, but it does not report per-arm numbers analysed at 8 or 16 weeks for the outcomes, nor actual post-randomization losses/withdrawals, reasons for missing outcome data, or a CONSORT flow with analysed denominators. Stating ITT/planned mixed models without observed attrition numbers is insufficient to judge Low or High; therefore attrition bias is unclear.
